title: Cybersecurity Analyst
description: Protect systems and networks from security threats. Learn networking, Linux, scripting, web security, cryptography, penetration testing tools, and industry certifications.

nodes.title: Networking Fundamentals
nodes.description: TCP/IP, OSI model, DNS, HTTP/HTTPS, firewalls, VPNs, and network protocols.

nodes.title: Linux for Security
nodes.description: Linux administration, file permissions, process management, log analysis, and command-line proficiency.

nodes.title: Scripting (Python/Bash)
nodes.description: Python and Bash scripting for automation, log analysis, and security tooling.

nodes.title: Web Application Security
nodes.description: OWASP Top 10 — SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, SSRF, authentication flaws, and API security.

nodes.title: Cryptography
nodes.description: Symmetric/asymmetric encryption, hashing, digital signatures, PKI, TLS/SSL, and certificate management.

nodes.title: Reconnaissance & OSINT
nodes.description: Information gathering — WHOIS, DNS enumeration, Shodan, Google dorking, social engineering, and passive reconnaissance.

nodes.title: Vulnerability Assessment
nodes.description: Identify and classify vulnerabilities using Nessus, OpenVAS, and manual techniques.

nodes.title: Penetration Testing
nodes.description: Methodology: planning, scanning, exploitation, post-exploitation, and reporting. Hands-on with Metasploit, Burp Suite.

nodes.title: Security Tools
nodes.description: Master essential tools — Nmap, Wireshark, Burp Suite, John the Ripper, Hashcat, and Nikto.

nodes.title: Network Security
nodes.description: Firewalls, IDS/IPS, VPN configuration, network segmentation, and intrusion detection.

nodes.title: Incident Response
nodes.description: Incident handling process — preparation, detection, containment, eradication, recovery, and lessons learned.

nodes.title: Digital Forensics
nodes.description: Disk forensics, memory forensics, network forensics, and forensic toolkits (Autopsy, Volatility).

nodes.title: Malware Analysis
nodes.description: Static and dynamic malware analysis, reverse engineering basics, sandboxing, and threat intelligence.

nodes.title: Cloud Security
nodes.description: AWS/GCP/Azure security — IAM, security groups, encryption, compliance, and cloud-specific vulnerabilities.

nodes.title: Certifications
nodes.description: CompTIA Security+, CEH, OSCP, CISSP — plan your certification path and prepare.

nodes.title: CTF Challenges
nodes.description: Practice with Capture The Flag competitions — picoCTF, HackerOne CTF, and online CTF platforms.

nodes.title: Social Engineering
nodes.description: Phishing, pretexting, baiting, tailgating, and social engineering defense techniques.

nodes.title: Security Reporting
nodes.description: Writing security reports, vulnerability disclosures, risk assessments, and communicating findings to stakeholders.

nodes.title: IoT Security
nodes.description: Securing embedded devices, firmware analysis, network segmentation, and IoT-specific attack vectors.

nodes.title: Wireless Security
nodes.description: WiFi security protocols (WPA2/3), wireless attacks, Evil Twin, and packet analysis with Aircrack-ng.

nodes.title: SIEM & Log Analysis
nodes.description: Security Information and Event Management — ELK, Splunk (free tier), Wazuh, and log analysis techniques.
